915	to the open-immersion adjunction `f.opensFunctor ⊣ Opens.map f.base` it gives
916	`pushforward β ⊣ pushforward φ`. -/
917	noncomputable def pushforwardPushforwardAdj : pushforward.{u} φ ⊣ pushforward.{u} ψ where
918	  unit :=
919	    (pushforwardId _).inv ≫ pushforwardNatTrans (𝟙 _) adj.counit ≫
920	      (pushforwardCongr (by simpa using H₁)).hom ≫ (pushforwardComp _ _).inv
921	  counit :=
922	    (pushforwardComp _ _).hom ≫ pushforwardNatTrans _ adj.unit ≫
923	      (pushforwardCongr (by simpa using H₂)).hom ≫ (pushforwardId _).hom
924	  left_triangle_components X := by
925	    ext U x
926	    change (X.presheaf.map (adj.counit.app (F.obj U.unop)).op ≫
927	      X.presheaf.map (F.map (adj.unit.app U.unop)).op) _ = _
928	    dsimp only [id_obj]
929	    rw [← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, adj.left_triangle_components]
930	    simp
931	  right_triangle_components X := by
932	    ext U x
933	    change (X.presheaf.map (G.map (adj.counit.app U.unop)).op ≫
934	      X.presheaf.map (adj.unit.app (G.obj U.unop)).op) _ = _
935	    rw [← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, adj.right_triangle_components]
936	    simp
937	
938	end PushforwardAdj
939	
940	section StrongMonoidalRestrictScalars
941	
942	universe v'
943	
944	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v'} C]
945	
946	/-- **A sectionwise-isomorphism morphism of presheaves of modules is an isomorphism.**
947	The inverse is assembled sectionwise via `isoMk` (whose forward naturality is exactly the
948	naturality of the given morphism). -/
949	lemma isIso_of_isIso_app {𝓡 : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} 𝓡}
950	    (g : M ⟶ N) (h : ∀ U, IsIso (g.app U)) : IsIso g := by
951	  haveI := h
952	  have hg : g = (PresheafOfModules.isoMk (fun U => asIso (g.app U))
953	      (fun _ _ φ => g.naturality φ)).hom :=
954	    PresheafOfModules.hom_ext (fun _ => rfl)
955	  rw [hg]; infer_instance
956	
957	variable {R S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
958	
959	/-- **`PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α` is strong monoidal when `α` is sectionwise
960	bijective.** The lax tensorator `μ` and unit `ε` (`restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal`) are assembled
961	sectionwise from the `ModuleCat`-level ones, which are isomorphisms for a bijective ground-ring
962	map (`restrictScalars_isIso_μ_of_bijective`, `restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective`); hence the
963	presheaf `μ`/`ε` are sectionwise isos, hence isos (`isIso_of_isIso_app`), and the lax structure
964	upgrades to strong via `Functor.Monoidal.ofLaxMonoidal`. This is the **H2** presheaf lift of
965	`tensorObj_restrict_iso`. -/
966	@[implicit_reducible]
967	noncomputable def restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective
968	    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
969	    (hα : ∀ U, Function.Bijective (α.app U).hom) :
970	    (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).Monoidal := by
971	  haveI hε : IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α)) :=
972	    isIso_of_isIso_app _ (fun U => restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective (α.app U).hom (hα U))
973	  haveI hμ : ∀ M₁ M₂, IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α) M₁ M₂) :=
974	    fun M₁ M₂ => isIso_of_isIso_app _
975	      (fun U => restrictScalars_isIso_μ_of_bijective (α.app U).hom (hα U) (M₁.obj U) (M₂.obj U))
976	  exact Functor.Monoidal.ofLaxMonoidal _
977	
978	end StrongMonoidalRestrictScalars

980	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the internal hom of presheaves of modules
981	(slice formula): the `R(T)`-module structure on `Hom(M, N)`
982	
983	This section builds the FIRST primitive of the sheaf internal-hom / dual block
984	(blueprint `sec:tensorobj_dual_infra`, the `⊗`-inverse's missing ingredient): the
985	`R(T)`-module structure on the morphism abelian group `M ⟶ N` of presheaves of
986	modules over a base category `C` with a **terminal object** `T`, where the scalar
987	ring is the global ring `R(T)`.
988	
989	This is exactly the module attached to each value of the slice internal hom
990	`ℋom(M, N)(U) := ModuleCat.of (R(U)) (M|_U ⟶ N|_U)` of
991	blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom`: over the restricted site (terminal `U`),
992	the section module over `U` is `Hom(M|_U, N|_U)` with its `R(U)`-action. The slice
993	formula is forced by contravariance of the naive pointwise rule
994	`U ↦ Hom_{R(U)}(M(U), N(U))`; the module of morphisms of *restricted* objects is
995	the covariant remedy, and its `R(U)`-module structure is the content here.
996	
997	The action is `f • φ := φ ≫ globalSMul f`, where `globalSMul f : N ⟶ N` is the
998	"multiply by the global scalar `f ∈ R(T)`" endomorphism: at an object `Y`, with
999	`R(T) → R(Y)` the canonical map `termRingMap` induced by the unique `Y → T`, it is
1000	scalar multiplication by the image of `f`. Mathlib has the fixed-ring internal hom
1001	`ihom M N = (M ⟶ N)` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Monoidal/Closed.lean`) but
1002	nothing for the varying structure sheaf at the `PresheafOfModules` level; this is the
1003	project-local supplement. -/
1004	
1005	namespace InternalHom
1006	
1007	open CategoryTheory Limits
1008	
1009	universe vC uC
1010	
1011	variable {C : Type uC} [Category.{vC} C] {R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
1012	  {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T)
1013	
1014	/-- **The canonical ring map `R(T) → R(Y)` from a terminal object `T`.** For each
1015	object `Y`, the unique morphism `Y.unop → T` (terminality) induces, after `op` and
1016	applying `R`, the ring map `R(T) → R(Y)` along which a global scalar `f ∈ R(T)` acts
1017	on `R(Y)`-modules. Project-local: the `R(T)`-module structure on `Hom(M, N)` (the slice
1018	internal-hom value) is defined through this map. -/
1019	noncomputable def termRingMap (Y : Cᵒᵖ) : R.obj (Opposite.op T) ⟶ R.obj Y :=
1020	  R.map (hT.from Y.unop).op
1021	
1022	/-- **Naturality of `termRingMap`.** The restriction map `R(g) : R(X) → R(Y)` of `R`
1023	along `g : X ⟶ Y` carries `termRingMap X f` to `termRingMap Y f`; equivalently the
1024	images of a global scalar `f ∈ R(T)` are compatible with restriction. This is the
1025	sole ingredient making `globalSMul f` a genuine morphism of presheaves of modules. -/
1026	lemma termRingMap_naturality {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (g : X ⟶ Y) (f) :
1027	    (ConcreteCategory.hom ((R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).map g))
1028	        ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT X)) f)
1029	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f := by
1030	  have h : (hT.from X.unop).op ≫ g = (hT.from Y.unop).op := by
1031	    apply Quiver.Hom.unop_inj; apply hT.hom_ext
1032	  change (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map g)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map (hT.from X.unop).op)) f) = _
1033	  rw [show termRingMap hT Y = R.map (hT.from Y.unop).op from rfl, ← h, Functor.map_comp]; rfl
1034	
1035	/-- **Multiplication by a global scalar `f ∈ R(T)` as an endomorphism of `N`.** At an
1036	object `Y`, it is scalar multiplication by `termRingMap Y f ∈ R(Y)` on `N(Y)`; the
1037	naturality square commutes by `termRingMap_naturality` and the semilinearity of the
1038	restriction maps of `N` (`PresheafOfModules.map_smul`). This is the central object: it
1039	gives the `R(T)`-action on `Hom(M, N)` by post-composition (`homModule`). -/
1040	noncomputable def globalSMul (N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
1041	    (f : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) : N ⟶ N :=
1042	  PresheafOfModules.Hom.mk
1043	    (fun Y => ModuleCat.ofHom
1044	      (LinearMap.lsmul (R.obj Y : Type u) (N.obj Y) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f)))
1045	    (by
1046	      intro X Y g; ext m
1047	      change (LinearMap.lsmul (R.obj Y : Type u) (N.obj Y)
1048	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (N.map g)) m)
1049	          = (ConcreteCategory.hom (N.map g)) ((LinearMap.lsmul (R.obj X : Type u) (N.obj X)
1050	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT X)) f)) m)
1051	      rw [LinearMap.lsmul_apply, LinearMap.lsmul_apply]; erw [PresheafOfModules.map_smul]
1052	      rw [termRingMap_naturality]; rfl)
1053	
1054	variable {N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
1055	
1056	/-- The section-wise action of `globalSMul f`: at `Y`, it is scalar multiplication by
1057	`termRingMap Y f`. -/
1058	lemma globalSMul_hom_apply (f : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) (Y : Cᵒᵖ) (m : N.obj Y) :
1059	    ((globalSMul hT N f).app Y).hom m = ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f) • m := rfl
1060	
1061	/-- `globalSMul 1 = 𝟙`. -/
1062	lemma globalSMul_one : globalSMul hT N 1 = 𝟙 N := by
1063	  ext Y m; rw [globalSMul_hom_apply, map_one, one_smul]; rfl
1064	
1065	/-- `globalSMul 0 = 0`. -/
1066	lemma globalSMul_zero : globalSMul hT N 0 = 0 := by
1067	  ext Y m; rw [globalSMul_hom_apply, map_zero, zero_smul]; rfl
1068	
1069	/-- `globalSMul` is additive in the scalar. -/
1070	lemma globalSMul_add (f g : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) :
1071	    globalSMul hT N (f + g) = globalSMul hT N f + globalSMul hT N g := by
1072	  ext Y m; rw [add_app, globalSMul_hom_apply, _root_.map_add, add_smul]; rfl
1073	
1074	/-- `globalSMul` turns ring multiplication into composition (the scalar endomorphisms
1075	commute, so the order is immaterial). -/
1076	lemma globalSMul_mul (f g : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) :
1077	    globalSMul hT N (f * g) = globalSMul hT N g ≫ globalSMul hT N f := by
1078	  ext Y m; rw [comp_app, globalSMul_hom_apply, map_mul, mul_smul, ModuleCat.hom_comp,
1079	    LinearMap.comp_apply, globalSMul_hom_apply, globalSMul_hom_apply]
1080	
1081	/-- **The `R(T)`-module structure on `Hom(M, N)` of presheaves of modules over a base
1082	category `C` with a terminal object `T`.** The action `f • φ := φ ≫ globalSMul f` scales
1083	a morphism by post-composing with multiplication by the global scalar `f ∈ R(T)`; the
1084	module axioms reduce to the `globalSMul_{one,zero,add,mul}` ring-homomorphism facts and
1085	the bilinearity of composition (`Preadditive`). This is the module carried by each value
1086	of the slice internal hom `ℋom(M, N)(U)` of blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom`
1087	(take `C =` the restricted site over `U`, with terminal `U`, so `R(T) = R(U)`).
1088	Project-local: Mathlib has the fixed-ring internal hom but no `PresheafOfModules`-level
1089	internal hom for the varying structure sheaf. -/
1090	@[implicit_reducible]
1091	noncomputable def homModule (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1092	    Module (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u) (M ⟶ N) where
1093	  smul f φ := φ ≫ globalSMul hT N f
1094	  one_smul φ := by change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N 1 = φ; rw [globalSMul_one, Category.comp_id]
1095	  mul_smul f g φ := by
1096	    change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N (f * g) = (φ ≫ globalSMul hT N g) ≫ globalSMul hT N f
1097	    rw [globalSMul_mul, Category.assoc]
1098	  smul_zero f := by change (0 : M ⟶ N) ≫ globalSMul hT N f = 0; rw [Limits.zero_comp]
1099	  zero_smul φ := by change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N 0 = 0; rw [globalSMul_zero, Limits.comp_zero]
1100	  smul_add f φ ψ := by
1101	    change (φ + ψ) ≫ globalSMul hT N f = φ ≫ globalSMul hT N f + ψ ≫ globalSMul hT N f
1102	    rw [Preadditive.add_comp]
1103	  add_smul f g φ := by
1104	    change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N (f + g) = φ ≫ globalSMul hT N f + φ ≫ globalSMul hT N g
1105	    rw [globalSMul_add, Preadditive.comp_add]
1106	
1107	/-! ### The slice value at an object `U` via the over-category `Over U`
1108	
1109	The slice internal hom `ℋom(M, N)(U) := ModuleCat.of (R(U)) (M|_U ⟶ N|_U)` of
1110	blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom` is realized object-by-object here. The
1111	restriction `M|_U` is `PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀` along the over-category
1112	projection `Over.forget U : Over U ⥤ C`; the over-category has terminal object
1113	`Over.mk (𝟙 U)` (`Over.mkIdTerminal`), at which the restricted ring is `R(U)`
1114	(by `rfl`), so the `R(U)`-module structure on `M|_U ⟶ N|_U` is exactly `homModule`
1115	applied to that terminal. -/
1116	
1117	/-- **Restriction of a presheaf of modules to the over-category `Over U`.** This is
1118	the `M|_U` of the slice internal-hom formula: `PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀` along
1119	the over-category projection `Over.forget U`. The new base presheaf of rings is
1120	`(Over.forget U).op ⋙ R`, whose value at the terminal `Over.mk (𝟙 U)` is `R(U)`. -/
1121	noncomputable def restr (U : C)
1122	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1123	    PresheafOfModules.{u} (((Over.forget U).op ⋙ R) ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
1124	  (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.forget U) (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)).obj M
1125	
1126	/-- **The slice internal-hom value at `U`: `Hom(M|_U, N|_U)` as an `R(U)`-module.**
1127	This is the `R(U)`-module underlying `ℋom(M, N)(U)` of blueprint
1128	`def:presheaf_internal_hom`: the morphism group of the over-category restrictions
1129	`M|_U ⟶ N|_U`, equipped with the `homModule` action for the terminal object
1130	`Over.mk (𝟙 U)` of `Over U`. The full presheaf `internalHom` (the assembly of these
1131	values over the restriction maps `V ⟶ U`) was assembled iter-220 (`internalHom`). -/
1132	@[implicit_reducible]
1133	noncomputable def internalHomObjModule (U : C)
1134	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1135	    Module (R.obj (Opposite.op U) : Type u) (restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :=
1136	  homModule (R := (Over.forget U).op ⋙ R) (T := Over.mk (𝟙 U)) Over.mkIdTerminal
1137	    (restr U M) (restr U N)
1138	
1139	/-- **The restriction map of the presheaf internal hom.** For a morphism `g : V ⟶ U`
1140	of `C`, restricting a morphism `φ : M|_U ⟶ N|_U` of restricted presheaves of modules
1141	along `Over.map g : Over V ⥤ Over U` yields a morphism `M|_V ⟶ N|_V`. Realised as
1142	`(pushforward₀ (Over.map g) …).map φ`; the target base ring presheaf
1143	`(Over.map g).op ⋙ (Over.forget U).op ⋙ R` is definitionally `(Over.forget V).op ⋙ R`,
1144	so the result has the expected type `restr V M ⟶ restr V N`. This is the
1145	further-restriction map `φ ↦ φ|_V` of blueprint `lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`. -/
1146	noncomputable def restrictionMap {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
1147	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
1148	    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) : restr V M ⟶ restr V N :=
1149	  (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.map g)
1150	    ((Over.forget U).op ⋙ (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).map φ
1151	
1152	/-- **`restrictionMap` is additive.** Part of the additivity assertion of blueprint
1153	`lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`. -/
1154	lemma restrictionMap_add {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
1155	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
1156	    (φ ψ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :
1157	    restrictionMap g (φ + ψ) = restrictionMap g φ + restrictionMap g ψ := by
1158	  ext1 X; rfl
1159	
1160	/-- **`restrictionMap` preserves zero.** -/
1161	lemma restrictionMap_zero {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
1162	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)} :
1163	    restrictionMap g (0 : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) = 0 := by
1164	  ext1 X; rfl
1165	
1166	/-- Helper: the component of a morphism of presheaves of modules at two *equal*
1167	objects agrees up to `HEq`. Proven by `subst`. Used to discharge the
1168	pseudofunctoriality coherence of `restrictionMap` (`Over.map` is only functorial in
1169	its argument up to `Over.mapId_eq` / `Over.mapComp_eq`). -/
1170	private lemma hom_app_heq {B : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} B}
1171	    (φ : M ⟶ N) {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (h : X = Y) : HEq (φ.app X) (φ.app Y) := by
1172	  subst h; rfl
1173	
1174	/-- **Functoriality of `restrictionMap`: identity.** -/
1175	lemma restrictionMap_id {U : C}
1176	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
1177	    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :
1178	    restrictionMap (𝟙 U) φ = φ := by
1179	  ext1 X
1180	  exact eq_of_heq (hom_app_heq φ (by rw [Over.mapId_eq]; rfl))
1181	
1182	/-- **Functoriality of `restrictionMap`: composition.** For `g : V ⟶ U`, `h : W ⟶ V`,
1183	restricting along `h ≫ g` is restricting along `g` then along `h`. -/
1184	lemma restrictionMap_comp {U V W : C} (g : V ⟶ U) (h : W ⟶ V)
1185	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
1186	    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :
1187	    restrictionMap (h ≫ g) φ = restrictionMap h (restrictionMap g φ) := by
1188	  ext1 X
1189	  exact eq_of_heq (hom_app_heq φ (by rw [Over.mapComp_eq]; rfl))
1190	
1191	/-- **`restrictionMap` respects composition of morphisms** (functoriality of
1192	`pushforward₀.map`). -/
1193	lemma restrictionMap_comp_hom {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
1194	    {M N P : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
1195	    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) (ψ : restr U N ⟶ restr U P) :
1196	    restrictionMap g (φ ≫ ψ) = restrictionMap g φ ≫ restrictionMap g ψ :=
1197	  Functor.map_comp _ _ _
1198	
1199	/-- **Restriction commutes with the global-scalar endomorphism.** For `g : V ⟶ U`
1200	and a global scalar `r ∈ R(U)`, restricting the multiplication-by-`r` endomorphism of
1201	`N|_U` yields the multiplication-by-`R(g)(r)` endomorphism of `N|_V`. This is the heart
1202	of the semilinearity of `restrictionMap` (blueprint `lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`):
1203	both sides act on each section by scalar multiplication, and the scalars agree by
1204	functoriality of `R` (`termRingMap_naturality`). -/
1205	lemma restrictionMap_globalSMul {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
1206	    {N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
1207	    (r : (R.obj (Opposite.op U) : Type u)) :
1208	    restrictionMap g (globalSMul (R := (Over.forget U).op ⋙ R) (T := Over.mk (𝟙 U))
1209	        Over.mkIdTerminal (restr U N) r)
1210	      = globalSMul (R := (Over.forget V).op ⋙ R) (T := Over.mk (𝟙 V))
1211	        Over.mkIdTerminal (restr V N) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map g.op)) r) := by
1212	  ext1 Y
1213	  ext m
1214	  rw [globalSMul_hom_apply]
1215	  erw [globalSMul_hom_apply]
1216	  congr 1
1217	  simp only [termRingMap, Functor.comp_map]
1218	  have hmor : (Over.forget U).op.map
1219	        (Over.mkIdTerminal.from ((Over.map g).op.obj Y).unop).op
1220	      = g.op ≫ (Over.forget V).op.map (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop).op := by
1221	    apply Quiver.Hom.unop_inj
1222	    change Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from ((Over.map g).obj Y.unop))
1223	      = Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop) ≫ g
1224	    have e1 : Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from ((Over.map g).obj Y.unop))
1225	        = ((Over.map g).obj Y.unop).hom := by simp
1226	    have e2 : Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop) = Y.unop.hom := by simp
1227	    rw [e1, e2]
1228	    simp [Over.map_obj_hom]
1229	  rw [hmor]
1230	  erw [← CommRingCat.comp_apply, ← R.map_comp]
1231	  rfl
1232	
1233	/-- **`restrictionMap` packaged as an additive group homomorphism** on the morphism
1234	groups, using `restrictionMap_zero` and `restrictionMap_add`. This is the value of the
1235	underlying `Ab`-presheaf morphism of `internalHom`. -/
1236	noncomputable def restrictionMapAddHom {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
1237	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1238	    (restr U M ⟶ restr U N) →+ (restr V M ⟶ restr V N) where
1239	  toFun := restrictionMap g
1240	  map_zero' := restrictionMap_zero g
1241	  map_add' := restrictionMap_add g
1242	
1243	/-- **The underlying `Ab`-valued presheaf of `internalHom M N`**: `U ↦ (M|_U ⟶ N|_U)`
1244	with the further-restriction maps. Functoriality is `restrictionMap_id` /
1245	`restrictionMap_comp`. This is piece (a)+(b)+(c) of blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom`
1246	at the abelian-group level; the `R(U)`-module refinement is added by `internalHom`. -/
1247	noncomputable def internalHomPresheaf
1248	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1249	    Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{max u uC vC} where
1250	  obj X := AddCommGrpCat.of (restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop N)
1251	  map {X Y} f := AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (restrictionMapAddHom f.unop M N)
1252	  map_id X := by
1253	    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
1254	    refine AddMonoidHom.ext fun φ => ?_
1255	    exact restrictionMap_id φ
1256	  map_comp {X Y Z} f f' := by
1257	    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
1258	    refine AddMonoidHom.ext fun φ => ?_
1259	    exact restrictionMap_comp f.unop f'.unop φ
1260	
1261	/-- **Semilinearity of `restrictionMap`** in the form consumed by
1262	`PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf`: for `f : X ⟶ Y` in `Cᵒᵖ`, a global scalar
1263	`r ∈ R(X)` and `m : M|_{X} ⟶ N|_{X}`, `restrictionMap f.unop (r • m) =
1264	R(f)(r) • restrictionMap f.unop m`, where the `•`'s are the slice-value module
1265	actions (`internalHomObjModule`). This is `lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`'s
1266	compatibility datum; it follows from `restrictionMap_comp_hom` and
1267	`restrictionMap_globalSMul`. -/
1268	lemma restrictionMap_smul {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y)
1269	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
1270	    (r : (R.obj X : Type u)) (m : restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop N) :
1271	    restrictionMap f.unop
1272	        (letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop M N; r • m)
1273	      = (letI := internalHomObjModule Y.unop M N;
1274	          (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map f)) r • restrictionMap f.unop m) := by
1275	  change restrictionMap f.unop (m ≫ globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal (restr X.unop N) r)
1276	    = restrictionMap f.unop m
1277	        ≫ globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal (restr Y.unop N) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map f)) r)
1278	  rw [restrictionMap_comp_hom, restrictionMap_globalSMul, Quiver.Hom.op_unop]
1279	
1280	end InternalHom
1281	
1282	/-! ### Assembly of the presheaf internal hom over a single-universe base
1283	
1284	`PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf` ties the underlying `Ab`-presheaf to the ground ring
1285	presheaf `R`'s universe (`Type u`), but for a general base category the morphism groups
1286	`M|_U ⟶ N|_U` live in `Type (max u uC vC)`. The two coincide exactly when the base
1287	category is single-universe (`Category.{u, u}`), which is the case for the topological
1288	site `Opens X` underlying the structure sheaf of a scheme. The full presheaf internal
1289	hom is therefore assembled in this specialised universe context. -/
1290	
1291	namespace InternalHom
1292	
1293	section Assembly
1294	
1295	variable {D : Type u} [Category.{u, u} D] {R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
1296	
1297	/-- **The presheaf internal hom `ℋom(M, N)`** (blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom`):
1298	the presheaf of `R`-modules assembled from the slice value modules
1299	`internalHomObjModule` (a), the further-restriction maps `internalHomPresheaf` (b)(c),
1300	and the semilinearity datum `restrictionMap_smul`, via `PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf`.
1301	Stated over a single-universe base (e.g. the topological site `Opens X`); see the
1302	section note for the universe constraint. -/
1303	noncomputable def internalHom
1304	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1305	    PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
1306	  @PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf D _ (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
1307	    (internalHomPresheaf M N)
1308	    (fun X => internalHomObjModule X.unop M N)
1309	    (fun {_ _} f r m => restrictionMap_smul f M N r m)
1310	
1311	end Assembly
1312	
1313	end InternalHom
1314	
1315	namespace InternalHom
1316	
1317	open CategoryTheory Limits Opposite
1318	
1319	universe vC uC
1320	
1321	variable {C : Type uC} [Category.{vC} C] {R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
1322	  {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T)
1323	
1324	/-- **`termRingMap` at the terminal object itself is the identity.** The unique
1325	`T → T` is the identity, so the induced ring map `R(T) → R(T)` is `id`. This is the
1326	fact that the global-scalar action evaluated at the terminal section is the scalar
1327	itself, used to prove the evaluation map of `internalHomEval` is `R(U)`-bilinear. -/
1328	lemma termRingMap_terminal (f : (R.obj (op T) : Type u)) :
1329	    (ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT (op T))) f = f := by
1330	  have hid : hT.from T = 𝟙 T := hT.hom_ext _ _
1331	  simp only [termRingMap, unop_op, hid, op_id, R.map_id]
1332	  rfl
1333	
1334	end InternalHom
1335	
1336	/-! ### The presheaf dual `M^∨ := ℋom(M, R)`
1337	
1338	The presheaf dual is the internal hom into the monoidal unit (the structure presheaf
1339	viewed as a module over itself), assembled in the same single-universe context as
1340	`InternalHom.internalHom`. Blueprint `def:presheaf_dual`. -/
1341	
1342	section Dual
1343	
1344	open InternalHom Opposite
1345	
1346	variable {D : Type u} [Category.{u, u} D] {R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
1347	
1348	/-- **The presheaf dual `M^∨ := ℋom(M, R)`** (blueprint `def:presheaf_dual`): the
1349	internal hom into the monoidal unit `𝟙_` (the structure presheaf `R` viewed as a module
1350	over itself / the regular representation). The value
1351	`M^∨(U) = ModuleCat.of (R(U)) (M|_U ⟶ R|_U)` is the `R(U)`-module of `R|_U`-linear
1352	functionals on `M|_U`, the presheaf-of-modules analogue of the fixed-ring linear dual
1353	`Module.Dual R M = (M ⟶ R)` that serves as the `⊗`-inverse of an invertible module.
1354	Project-local: Mathlib has the fixed-ring dual but no `PresheafOfModules`-level dual for
1355	the varying structure sheaf. -/
1356	noncomputable def dual
1357	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1358	    PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
1359	  InternalHom.internalHom M (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
1360	
1361	/-- The evaluation functional `φ ↦ (s ↦ φ(s))` of a dual section `φ`, cast to an
1362	`R₀.obj X`-linear map `M(X) → R(X)` (the over-category ring at the terminal section is
1363	definitionally `R₀.obj X`). Helper for `internalHomEvalApp`. -/
1364	noncomputable def evalLin
1365	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) (X : Dᵒᵖ)
1366	    (φ : (dual M).obj X) :
1367	    (M.obj X : Type u) →ₗ[(R₀.obj X : Type u)] (R₀.obj X : Type u) :=
1368	  ((φ : restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop
1369	      (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).app
1370	        (op (Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)))).hom
1371	
1372	/-- `evalLin` is additive in the dual section `φ` (the dual-section addition is the
1373	categorical `Hom`-addition, on which `app`/`hom`-application is definitionally additive). -/
1374	lemma evalLin_add
1375	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) (X : Dᵒᵖ)
1376	    (φ φ' : (dual M).obj X) :
1377	    evalLin M X (φ + φ') = evalLin M X φ + evalLin M X φ' :=
1378	  LinearMap.ext fun _ => rfl
1379	
1380	/-- `evalLin` is `R₀.obj X`-linear in the dual section `φ`, using the `homModule`
1381	action `c • φ = φ ≫ globalSMul c` and `termRingMap_terminal`. -/
1382	lemma evalLin_smul
1383	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) (X : Dᵒᵖ)
1384	    (c : (R₀.obj X : Type u)) (φ : (dual M).obj X) :
1385	    evalLin M X (c • φ) = c • evalLin M X φ := by
1386	  refine LinearMap.ext fun s => ?_
1387	  rw [LinearMap.smul_apply]
1388	  change ((ConcreteCategory.hom
1389	      (termRingMap (R := (Over.forget X.unop).op ⋙ R₀) Over.mkIdTerminal
1390	        (op (Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)))) c)
1391	    • ((φ : restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop
1392	        (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).app
1393	          (op (Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)))).hom s : (R₀.obj X : Type u))
1394	    = c • evalLin M X φ s
1395	  rw [termRingMap_terminal]
1396	  rfl
1397	
1398	/-- **The open-by-open evaluation/contraction map** underlying `internalHomEval`. At an
1399	object `X` it is the `R(X)`-bilinear contraction
1400	`(M(X)) ⊗_{R(X)} (M|_X ⟶ R|_X) → R(X)`, `s ⊗ φ ↦ φ(s)`, where `φ(s)` evaluates the
1401	functional `φ` (a morphism of restricted presheaves of modules over `Over X.unop`) at its
1402	terminal section, applied to `s`. Bilinearity over `R(X)`: linearity in `s` is linearity
1403	of `evalLin`; linearity in `φ` is `evalLin_add` / `evalLin_smul`. The codomain is cast to
1404	`R₀.obj X` (defeq to the unit value `(𝟙_).obj X`) to pin the `CommRingCat` module. -/
1405	noncomputable def internalHomEvalApp
1406	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) (X : Dᵒᵖ) :
1407	    (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M (dual M)).obj X ⟶
1408	      (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).obj X :=
1409	  show ModuleCat.of (R₀.obj X : Type u)
1410	        (TensorProduct (R₀.obj X : Type u) (M.obj X) ((dual M).obj X))
1411	      ⟶ ModuleCat.of (R₀.obj X : Type u) (R₀.obj X : Type u) from
1412	  ModuleCat.ofHom
1413	    (TensorProduct.lift
1414	      (LinearMap.mk₂ (R₀.obj X : Type u)
1415	        (fun (s : M.obj X) (φ : (dual M).obj X) => evalLin M X φ s)
1416	        (fun s s' φ => _root_.map_add (evalLin M X φ) s s')
1417	        (fun c s φ => _root_.map_smul (evalLin M X φ) c s)
1418	        (fun s φ φ' => by
1419	          change evalLin M X (φ + φ') s = evalLin M X φ s + evalLin M X φ' s
1420	          rw [evalLin_add, LinearMap.add_apply])
1421	        (fun c s φ => by
1422	          change evalLin M X (c • φ) s = c • evalLin M X φ s
1423	          rw [evalLin_smul, LinearMap.smul_apply])))
1424	
1425	/-- Value of `internalHomEvalApp` on a simple tensor: the contraction `s ⊗ φ ↦ φ(s)`.
1426	The eval value is kept at its NATURAL over-ring type `R₀.obj X` (not ascribed to the
1427	unit value `(𝟙_).obj X`). Used to reduce the naturality of `internalHomEval`. -/
1428	@[simp] lemma internalHomEvalApp_tmul
1429	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) (X : Dᵒᵖ)
1430	    (s : (M.obj X : Type u)) (φ : ((dual M).obj X : Type u)) :
1431	    (internalHomEvalApp M X).hom (s ⊗ₜ[(R₀.obj X : Type u)] φ) = evalLin M X φ s :=
1432	  rfl
1433	
1434	/-- **Further-restriction of `M|_U` along the canonical `Over.homMk f.unop` equals `M.map f`.**
1435	For `f : X ⟶ Y` in `Dᵒᵖ`, restricting `M|_{X.unop}` (the `pushforward₀` of `M` along
1436	`Over.forget X.unop`) along the over-category morphism `Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶
1437	Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)` is, definitionally, `M.map f`. Extracted as its own lemma so the heavy
1438	`whnf` defeq runs once within its own heartbeat budget (the `internalHomEval` naturality proof
1439	would otherwise time out). Used to rewrite the `naturality_apply` of a dual section into the
1440	shape `ev_M`'s naturality needs. -/
1441	private lemma restr_map_homMk
1442	    (N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) {X Y : Dᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y) :
1443	    (restr X.unop N).map (Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)).op
1444	      = N.map f := rfl
1445	
1446	/-- **The evaluation morphism `ev_M : M ⊗_R M^∨ ⟶ R`** (blueprint `lem:internal_hom_eval`):
1447	the full natural morphism `M ⊗_R M^∨ ⟶ R`, `s ⊗ φ ↦ φ(s)`, with `app X := internalHomEvalApp M X`.
1448	CLOSED axiom-clean iter-224 (`{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`). The per-object/value content
1449	is `internalHomEvalApp` + `internalHomEvalApp_tmul`; the `naturality` field is the six-step
1450	`evalLin`/`key`/`hdt` reduction recorded in the proof body. The iter-222/223 `whnf` HEARTBEAT-BOMB
1451	diagnosis (>200000 heartbeats forced by `kabstract` whnf-ing the monoidal unit `𝟙_`) turned out to be
1452	STALE after a Mathlib update: the composition now splits cleanly with
1453	`erw [ModuleCat.hom_comp, …]`, every elementwise rewrite (`internalHomEvalApp_tmul`,
1454	`PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply`, `restr_map_homMk`) fires without any bomb, and the dual-section
1455	naturality square closes directly. No transparency hacks (`with_reducible`), no `unit`-reshape, and no
1456	`maxHeartbeats` bump were needed. -/
1457	noncomputable def internalHomEval
1458	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1459	    PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M (dual M) ⟶
1460	      𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :=
1461	  PresheafOfModules.Hom.mk (fun X => internalHomEvalApp M X) (by
1462	    intro X Y f
1463	    refine ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun s φ => ?_)
1464	    -- iter-224: the iter-222/223 `whnf` HEARTBEAT-BOMB diagnosis (the codomain `𝟙_` forcing
1465	    -- `kabstract` to whnf the monoidal-unit machinery, >200000 heartbeats) turned out STALE after a
1466	    -- Mathlib update. The composition splits cleanly with `erw [ModuleCat.hom_comp, …]` and every
1467	    -- elementwise rewrite fires without any bomb; the six-step `evalLin`/`key`/`hdt` reduction below
1468	    -- goes through directly. No `with_reducible`, no `unit`-reshape, no `maxHeartbeats` bump.
1469	    -- Step 1: break the two `≫` on `s ⊗ₜ φ`, then `tensorObj_map_tmul` + `internalHomEvalApp_tmul`
1470	    -- (handled by `erw`'s defeq matching through `restrictScalars`):
1471	    --   LHS ⇒ `evalLin M Y ((dual M).map f φ) ((M.map f) s)`.
1472	    erw [ModuleCat.hom_comp, ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearMap.comp_apply,
1473	      internalHomEvalApp_tmul, internalHomEvalApp_tmul]
1474	    simp only []
1475	    -- Reduce the RHS `(𝟙_).map f`-image of `(internalHomEvalApp M X)(s ⊗ₜ φ)` to the unit ring
1476	    -- map applied to `evalLin M X φ s` (defeq, since `internalHomEvalApp_tmul` is `rfl`).
1477	    change M.evalLin Y ((M.dual.map f) φ) ((M.map f) s)
1478	      = ((𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).map f).hom
1479	          (M.evalLin X φ s)
1480	    -- Step 4: naturality of the dual section `φ` (a morphism over `Over X.unop`) along the
1481	    -- canonical `(Over.homMk f.unop).op : (terminal) ⟶ (op (Over.mk f.unop))`, applied to `s`.
1482	    have key := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply
1483	      (φ : restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop
1484	        (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))))
1485	      (Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)).op s
1486	    -- Both further-restrictions `(restr X.unop _).map (Over.homMk f.unop).op` are definitionally
1487	    -- the base maps `M.map f` / `(𝟙_).map f` (`restr_map_homMk`).
1488	    rw [restr_map_homMk, restr_map_homMk] at key
1489	    -- Step 5 (`hdt`): identify the dual section's terminal value with `φ` evaluated at
1490	    -- `op (Over.mk f.unop)` (the over-objects `Over.mk (𝟙 Y.unop ≫ f.unop)` and `Over.mk f.unop`
1491	    -- are equal via `Category.id_comp`, with equal source `Y.unop`, so `hom_app_heq`+`eq_of_heq`).
1492	    have hdt : M.evalLin Y ((M.dual.map f) φ) = (φ.app (op (Over.mk f.unop))).hom :=
1493	      congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom
1494	        (eq_of_heq (hom_app_heq
1495	          (φ : restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop
1496	            (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))))
1497	          (congrArg op (congrArg Over.mk (Category.id_comp f.unop)))))
1498	    -- Step 6: chain `hdt` (applied to `(M.map f) s`) into `key`.
1499	    exact (DFunLike.congr_fun hdt _).trans key
1500	    )
1501	
1502	end Dual
1503	
1504	end PresheafOfModules

1506	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
1507	
1508	namespace Scheme
1509	
1510	namespace Modules
1511	
1512	/-! ## §1. The substrate tensor-product operation -/
1513	
1514	/-- **The substrate operation `⊗` on `Scheme.Modules X`.**
1515	
1516	For a scheme `X` and `M, N : X.Modules`, the tensor product
1517	`M ⊗_X N : X.Modules` is the sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor
1518	product `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` of the underlying presheaves of
1519	`M` and `N` (affine-locally `(M ⊗_X N)(Spec A) = M(Spec A) ⊗_A N(Spec A)`).
1520	
1521	Per blueprint `def:scheme_modules_tensorobj`. The body lifts
1522	`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` through the sheafification functor on
1523	the small Zariski site of `X` (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
1524	noncomputable def tensorObj {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
1525	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
1526	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) M.val N.val) :
1527	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)
1528	
1529	/-- **Functoriality of `⊗_X`.**
1530	
1531	A pair of morphisms `f : M ⟶ M'` and `g : N ⟶ N'` in `X.Modules` determines a
1532	morphism `f ⊗ g : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N'`, compatible with identities
1533	and composition; the assignment `(M, N) ↦ tensorObj M N` thereby extends to a
1534	bifunctor `X.Modules × X.Modules ⥤ X.Modules` natural in both arguments.
1535	
1536	Per blueprint `lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality`. The body inherits
1537	the morphism action from `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` under
1538	sheafification (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
1539	noncomputable def tensorObj_functoriality {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
1540	    (f : M ⟶ M') (g : N ⟶ N') : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N' :=
1541	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
1542	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
1543	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) f.val g.val)
1544	
1545	/-- **`⊗`-invertibility of an `𝒪_X`-module.** (Blueprint
1546	`def:scheme_modules_isinvertible`.) `M : X.Modules` is `⊗`-invertible when it
1547	admits a tensor inverse: an object `N` with `M ⊗_X N ≅ 𝒪_X`, where
1548	`𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the designated unit. This is the
1549	scheme-level analogue of Mathlib's `Module.Invertible`; the predicate carries its
1550	inverse witness existentially, so the dual needed by the relative Picard group
1551	law is definitional. -/
1552	def IsInvertible {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : Prop :=
1553	  ∃ N : X.Modules, Nonempty (tensorObj M N ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
1554	
1555	/-- **The sheaf-level dual `M^∨ := ℋom_{𝒪_X}(M, 𝒪_X)`** of an `𝒪_X`-module.
1556	
1557	For a scheme `X` and `M : X.Modules`, the dual `dual M : X.Modules` is the
1558	sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules dual `PresheafOfModules.dual` of the
1559	underlying presheaf of `M` (the internal hom into the structure presheaf,
1560	`M^∨(U) = ℋom_{𝒪_X|_U}(M|_U, 𝒪_X|_U)`).
1561	
1562	Construction = the **exact dual analogue of `tensorObj`** (this file, `tensorObj`):
1563	apply the sheafification functor `PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 …)` on the
1564	small Zariski site of `X` to the (axiom-clean, sub-step-3) presheaf dual

1772	    (e : M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf) :
1773	    M.restrict W.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (W : Scheme).ringCatSheaf := by
1774	  have hWU' : W ≤ (𝟙 X) ⁻¹ᵁ U := hWU
1775	  set j : (W : Scheme) ⟶ (U : Scheme) := Scheme.Hom.resLE (𝟙 X) U W hWU' with hj
1776	  have hjι : j ≫ U.ι = W.ι := by rw [hj, Scheme.Hom.resLE_comp_ι, Category.comp_id]

1778	    CategoryTheory.final_of_representablyFlat _
1779	  -- M.restrict W.ι ≅ (pullback W.ι).obj M
1780	  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback W.ι).app M ≪≫ ?_
1781	  -- ≅ (pullback (j ≫ U.ι)).obj M
1782	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hjι.symm).app M ≪≫ ?_
1783	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj ((pullback U.ι).obj M)
1784	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp j U.ι).symm.app M ≪≫ ?_
1785	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj (M.restrict U.ι)

1787	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback U.ι).symm.app M) ≪≫ ?_
1788	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj 𝒪_U
1789	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullback j).mapIso e ≪≫ ?_
1790	  -- ≅ 𝒪_W
1791	  haveI hI : IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit j.toRingCatSheafHom) := inferInstance

1901	/-- **Tensor product of locally-trivial modules is locally trivial.**
1902	
1903	If `M, N : X.Modules` are locally trivial of rank one (line bundles), so is
1904	their tensor product `tensorObj M N`. Per blueprint
1905	`lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial`. The proof picks, for each point `x`,
1906	a common affine open `W ∋ x` contained in trivialising opens `U` (for `M`) and
1907	`U'` (for `N`), refines both trivialisations to `W` via `restrictIsoUnitOfLE`,
1908	then transports through `tensorObj_restrict_iso`, the bifunctoriality
1909	`tensorObjIsoOfIso`, and the unit isomorphism `tensorObj_unit_iso`:
1910	`(M ⊗ N)|_W ≅ M|_W ⊗ N|_W ≅ 𝒪_W ⊗ 𝒪_W ≅ 𝒪_W`. The only residual gap is the
1911	substrate-restriction compatibility `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. -/
1912	lemma tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
1913	    (hM : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial M) (hN : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial N) :
1914	    LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial (tensorObj M N) := by
1915	  intro x
1916	  obtain ⟨U, hxU, hU_aff, ⟨eM⟩⟩ := hM x
1917	  obtain ⟨U', hxU', hU'_aff, ⟨eN⟩⟩ := hN x
1918	  obtain ⟨W, hW_aff, hxW, hWsub⟩ :=
1919	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := X) (x := x) (U := U ⊓ U') ⟨hxU, hxU'⟩
1920	  have hWU : W ≤ U := le_trans hWsub inf_le_left
1921	  have hWU' : W ≤ U' := le_trans hWsub inf_le_right
1922	  refine ⟨W, hxW, hW_aff, ⟨?_⟩⟩
1923	  exact tensorObj_restrict_iso W.ι M N ≪≫
1924	    tensorObjIsoOfIso (restrictIsoUnitOfLE hWU eM) (restrictIsoUnitOfLE hWU' eN) ≪≫
1925	    tensorObj_unit_iso

1927	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the d.2-free descent re-route (B-connector)
1928	
1929	The "locally-iso ⇒ iso" half of the descent assembly of `exists_tensorObj_inverse`:
1930	a morphism of `𝒪_X`-modules that restricts to an isomorphism on an open
1931	neighbourhood of every point is a global isomorphism. The route is the stalkwise
1932	iso criterion `TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso` (for sheaves valued
1933	in `Ab`, whose forgetful functor reflects isos and preserves limits / filtered
1934	colimits) together with `Scheme.Modules.restrictStalkNatIso` (restriction along an
1935	open immersion commutes with stalks). **No stalk-⊗ ("d.2") is invoked**: this is a
1936	statement about a single module morphism, never about the tensor stalk. -/
1937	
1938	/-- **B-connector: a morphism of `𝒪_X`-modules that restricts to an isomorphism on
1939	an open cover is an isomorphism.** For `φ : M ⟶ N` in `X.Modules`, if every point
1940	`x` lies in an open `U x` on which the restriction `(restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ`
1941	is an isomorphism, then `φ` is an isomorphism. This is the B-bridge of the d.2-free
1942	descent re-route assembling `exists_tensorObj_inverse`. -/
1943	lemma isIso_of_isIso_restrict {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules} (φ : M ⟶ N)
1944	    (U : X → X.Opens) (hxU : ∀ x, x ∈ U x)
1945	    (h : ∀ x, IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ)) :
1946	    IsIso φ := by
1947	  -- It suffices that each stalk map of the underlying `Ab`-sheaf morphism is iso.
1948	  have hst : ∀ x : X, IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x).map
1949	      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ)) := by
1950	    intro x
1951	    obtain ⟨x', hx'⟩ : ∃ x', (U x).ι x' = x := by
1952	      have hmem : x ∈ (U x).ι.opensRange := by
1953	        rw [Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]; exact hxU x
1954	      exact AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.mem_opensRange.mp hmem
1955	    haveI : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ) := h x
1956	    -- `(restrictFunctor … ⋙ toPresheaf … ⋙ stalkFunctor x').map φ` is iso (functor of an iso).
1957	    haveI hFφ : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).ι ⋙
1958	        Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf _ ⋙ TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x').map φ) := by
1959	      dsimp only [Functor.comp_map]; exact Functor.map_isIso _ _
1960	    -- Transport the iso across `restrictStalkNatIso` to the stalk at `(U x).ι x' = x`.
1961	    have hGφ : IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} ((U x).ι x')).map
1962	        ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ)) :=
1963	      (CategoryTheory.NatIso.isIso_map_iff
1964	        (Scheme.Modules.restrictStalkNatIso (U x).ι x') φ).mp hFφ
1965	    exact hx' ▸ hGφ
1966	  -- Package as a morphism of `TopCat.Sheaf Ab X` and apply the stalkwise iso criterion.
1967	  let MS : TopCat.Sheaf Ab.{u} X := ⟨M.presheaf, M.isSheaf⟩
1968	  let NS : TopCat.Sheaf Ab.{u} X := ⟨N.presheaf, N.isSheaf⟩
1969	  let fS : MS ⟶ NS := ⟨(Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ⟩
1970	  haveI : ∀ x : X, IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x).map fS.hom) := hst
1971	  haveI hSiso : IsIso fS := TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso fS
1972	  have h1 : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ) := by
1973	    have := (TopCat.Sheaf.forget Ab.{u} X).map_isIso fS
1974	    exact this
1975	  exact (CategoryTheory.isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso φ (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X)).mp h1

1982	`lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible`. iter-206 flat-pivot: the designated unit is

2112	  %  \end{dfn}"
2113	  \textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 2,
2114	  Defs.~df:aPf + df:Pfs (the target category is the category of abelian
2115	  groups; the relative Picard functor is a quotient of abelian groups).}
2116	  Let \(C/k\) be a smooth proper geometrically integral curve over a
2117	  field \(k\), let \(\pi_C : C \to \Spec k\) be the structure morphism,
2118	  and let \(T\) be a \(k\)-scheme with structure morphism
2119	  \(\pi_T : T \to \Spec k\). The set
2120	  \[
2121	    \Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T) \;\;:=\;\; \Pic(C \times_k T) \,/\, \pi_T^*\Pic(T)
2122	  \]
2123	  of \cref{thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined} carries a canonical
2124	  abelian-group structure with addition
2125	  \(\bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] + \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]
2126	   := \bigl[\,\mathcal{L} \otimes_{C \times_k T} \mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]\),
2127	  neutral element \(\bigl[\,\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}\,\bigr]\), and
2128	  inverse
2129	  \(-\bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] := \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}^{-1}\,\bigr]\),
2130	  where \(\otimes_{C \times_k T}\) and \((-)^{-1}\) are the operations
2131	  supplied by the substrate of \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
2132	  restricted to the \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) carrier
2133	  via \cref{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}. With this structure the quotient
2134	  map
2135	  \(\Pic(C \times_k T) \twoheadrightarrow \Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T)\) is a
2136	  surjective group homomorphism with kernel exactly \(\pi_T^*\Pic(T)\),
2137	  in agreement with \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}.
2138	\end{theorem}
2139	
2140	\begin{proof}
2141	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct,
2142	        lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj,
2143	        def:scheme_modules_isinvertible,
2144	        lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup,
2145	        thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined,
2146	        lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}
2147	  Write \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) for the set of isomorphism classes of objects
2148	  of \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) (line bundles on
2149	  \(C \times_k T\)), with the operation
2150	  \([L] + [L'] := [L \otimes_{C \times_k T} L']\). This operation is
2151	  well-defined on isomorphism classes: an isomorphism \(L \cong L_0\) tensors
2152	  to an isomorphism \(L \otimes L' \cong L_0 \otimes L'\) by functoriality of
2153	  \(\otimes\) (\cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}), and
2154	  symmetrically in the second argument. The abelian-group structure on
2155	  \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) is then exactly the \emph{units group of the
2156	  commutative monoid of \(\otimes\)-iso-classes}
2157	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}): multiplication
2158	  \([L]\cdot[L'] = [L \otimes_{C\times_k T} L']\) is well-defined and
2159	  commutative-monoidal by the coherence isomorphisms
2160	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso},
2161	  and an iso-class is a unit precisely when its carrier is
2162	  \(\otimes\)-invertible (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}), the inverse
2163	  being carried by the predicate. Each axiom is a \emph{proposition} --- the
2164	  existence of an isomorphism --- so no monoidal-coherence datum (pentagon,
2165	  triangle, hexagon) is invoked; the \(\AddCommGroup\) on
2166	  \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) is the additive form of this units group. This mirrors
2167	  Mathlib's own Picard group: \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\,R\) carries its
2168	  \(\mathtt{CommGroup}\) (\(\mathtt{instCommGroupPic}\),
2169	  \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup}) transported from
2170	  \(\mathrm{Units}\bigl(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\,R)\bigr)\); here
2171	  the same \(\mathrm{Units}(\cdots)\) shape is transported to the scheme-level
2172	  \(\otimes_X\). (The scheme-level Picard group is genuinely absent from
2173	  Mathlib --- \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup} records ``connect to
2174	  invertible sheaves'' as an open task --- so the project supplies it lightly.)
2175	  The invertibility-predicate carrier is the project's \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\)
2176	  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}), the scheme-level analogue of
2177	  Mathlib's \(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\); the geometric predicate
2178	  \(\mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\) is retained separately and is connected to
2179	  \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\) only by an off-critical-path equivalence (only the
2180	  geometric \(\mathtt{IsInvertible} \Leftarrow \mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\)-style
2181	  direction is nontrivial, and it is not needed here).
2182	
2183	  By \cref{lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj}, the pullback functor
2184	  \(\pi_T^{*} : \texttt{LineBundle}\,T \to
2185	   \texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) carries tensor products to
2186	  tensor products and the unit to the unit, so the induced map on isomorphism
2187	  classes \(\pi_T^{*} : \Pic(T) \to \Pic(C \times_k T)\) is a group
2188	  homomorphism, and its image is a subgroup.
2189	
2190	  By \cref{thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined}, the underlying set of
2191	  \(\Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T)\) is the set of cosets of this subgroup. The
2192	  quotient of an abelian group by a subgroup is itself an abelian group, via
2193	  the standard \(\mathtt{QuotientAddGroup}\) construction, and the quotient
2194	  map is a surjective group homomorphism whose kernel is exactly the subgroup
2195	  (Stacks tag 01CR for the Picard-group special case; the general
2196	  quotient-of-abelian-groups statement is in any algebra textbook). Putting
2197	  the pieces together gives the \(\AddCommGroup\) instance on
2198	  \(\Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T)\) of the statement, and identifies it with the
2199	  structure of \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}.
2200	\end{proof}

2344	\section{Sheaf internal-hom of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules (the \(\otimes\)-inverse
2345	  infrastructure)}
2346	\label{sec:tensorobj_dual_infra}
2347	
2348	The PRIMARY group law (\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}) needs a
2349	\(\otimes\)-inverse for every line bundle, supplied by
2350	\cref{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible} as the dual
2351	\(L^{-1} = \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(L, \mathcal{O}_X)\). The standard route
2352	constructs that dual as a special case of the \emph{sheaf internal hom} of
2353	\(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules; over a fixed ring the internal hom of \(R\)-modules is the
2354	linear-coyoneda module \(\mathtt{ihom}\,M\,N = (M \to N)\), with dual
2355	\(\mathtt{ihom}\,M\,\mathbf{1} = M^\vee\), but for the \emph{varying} structure sheaf
2356	no such
2357	internal-hom / monoidal-closed structure exists at the
2358	\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\), \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules}\), or general-categorical
2359	level. This section decomposes the construction of the missing object into
2360	individually-formalizable sub-steps, following the slice presentation of the internal
2361	hom; each block names the intended Lean declaration and its dependencies. The
2362	specialisation that closes \cref{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible} is recorded in
2363	\cref{rem:dual_discharges_inverse}; an alternative descent-theoretic route is noted in
2364	\cref{rem:dual_via_stack}.
2365	
2366	A structural point governs the whole construction. The naive rule
2367	\(U \mapsto \Hom_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}(M(U), N(U))\) is \emph{contravariant} in the
2368	restriction maps of \(M\) (a restriction \(M(U) \to M(V)\) for \(V \subseteq U\)
2369	induces precomposition \(\Hom(M(V), N(V)) \to \Hom(M(U), \cdot)\) in the wrong
2370	direction), so it is \emph{not} a presheaf in the covariant sense and cannot be
2371	sheafified like the tensor product \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}. The remedy is
2372	the \emph{slice} formula \(U \mapsto (M|_U \to N|_U)\) --- the module of morphisms of
2373	\emph{restricted} modules over the restricted ring --- whose presheaf functoriality
2374	(restrict a global-over-\(U\) morphism to a global-over-\(V\) morphism, for
2375	\(V \subseteq U\)) \emph{is} covariant. Constructing that slice presheaf, and
2376	proving it satisfies the sheaf condition, is the substance of the build.
2377	
2378	\begin{definition}
2379	
2380	\leanok
2381	[The global-ring module on morphisms of presheaves of modules (value module)]
2382	  \label{def:presheaf_internal_hom_value}
2383	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.InternalHom.homModule}
2384	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
2385	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Modules on Ringed Spaces", \S Internal Hom
2386	  % (\texttt{section-internal-hom}, tag area 01CM)
2387	  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L3508--L3514).
2388	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
2389	  % "abelian groups. In addition, given an element
2390	  % $\varphi \in \Hom_{\mathcal{O}_X|_U}(\mathcal{F}|_U, \mathcal{G}|_U)$
2391	  % and a section $f \in \mathcal{O}_X(U)$ then we can define
2392	  % $f\varphi \in \Hom_{\mathcal{O}_X|_U}(\mathcal{F}|_U, \mathcal{G}|_U)$
2393	  % by either precomposing with multiplication by $f$ on $\mathcal{F}|_U$
2394	  % or postcomposing with multiplication by $f$ on $\mathcal{G}|_U$ (it gives
2395	  % the same result). Hence we in fact get a sheaf of $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules."
2396	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], ``Modules on Ringed Spaces'', \S Internal Hom
2397	  (tag area 01CM): the section ring acts on a morphism of restricted modules by
2398	  post-composition with multiplication by the section.}
2399	  Let \(R\) be a presheaf of commutative rings on a base category \(\mathcal{C}\)
2400	  that has a terminal object \(T\), and let \(M, N \in \mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R\).
2401	  The abelian group of morphisms \(M \longrightarrow N\) of presheaves of \(R\)-modules
2402	  carries a canonical \(R(T)\)-module structure --- the \emph{morphism module}
2403	  \(\mathtt{homModule}\,M\,N\). A global scalar \(f \in R(T)\) acts on a morphism
2404	  \(\varphi : M \to N\) by post-composition with multiplication by \(f\),
2405	  \[
2406	    f \cdot \varphi \;:=\; m_f^{N} \circ \varphi,
2407	  \]
2408	  where \(m_f^{N} : N \to N\) is the \emph{multiplication-by-\(f\)} endomorphism of
2409	  \(N\): the endomorphism that, on each object \(U \in \mathcal{C}\), is multiplication
2410	  by the restriction \(f|_U \in R(U)\) of \(f\) along the unique morphism \(U \to T\)
2411	  to the terminal object. The module axioms hold because the assignment
2412	  \(f \mapsto m_f^{N}\) is a ring homomorphism \(R(T) \to \mathrm{End}(N)\) into the
2413	  endomorphism ring of \(N\) --- a product \(f \cdot g\) goes to the \emph{composite}
2414	  \(m_f^{N} \circ m_g^{N}\) of the two multiplication endomorphisms, the order in which
2415	  the factors compose being reversed by the fact that the scalar acts by
2416	  \emph{post}-composition --- and composition of morphisms is biadditive in the
2417	  preadditive category \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R\). Distributivity and the
2418	  associativity of the action then follow from bilinearity of composition together with
2419	  the homomorphism property of \(f \mapsto m_f^{N}\). This is the genuinely new core of
2420	  the construction: Mathlib supplies the \emph{fixed}-ring internal hom
2421	  \(\mathtt{ihom}\,M\,N = (M \to N)\) over a single base ring, but nothing equipping the
2422	  morphism group with the module structure for the \emph{varying} structure presheaf at
2423	  the \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\) level.
2424	\end{definition}
2425	
2426	\begin{definition}
2427	
2428	\leanok
2429	[Slice specialisation of the morphism module]
2430	  \label{def:presheaf_internal_hom_slice_value}
2431	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.InternalHom.internalHomObjModule}
2432	  \uses{def:presheaf_internal_hom_value}
2433	  Specialise \cref{def:presheaf_internal_hom_value} to a slice category. For an object
2434	  (open) \(U\) of \(\mathcal{C} = \Opens X\), restricting along the forgetful functor
2435	  \(\mathtt{Over.forget}\,U : \mathcal{C}/U \to \mathcal{C}\) --- the open-immersion
2436	  restriction \(M \mapsto M|_U\) --- carries \(M, N\) to presheaves of modules
2437	  \(M|_U, N|_U\) over the restricted structure ring on the slice \(\mathcal{C}/U\). The
2438	  slice \(\mathcal{C}/U\) has the terminal object \(\mathtt{Over.mk}\,(\mathrm{id}_U)\),
2439	  at which the restricted ring takes the value \(R(U)\); hence
2440	  \cref{def:presheaf_internal_hom_value} makes the morphism group
2441	  \(M|_U \longrightarrow N|_U\) an \(R(U)\)-module, the
2442	  \(\mathtt{internalHomObjModule}\). This \(R(U)\)-module \((M|_U \to N|_U)\) is exactly
2443	  the value \(\mathcal{H}om(M, N)(U)\) of \cref{def:presheaf_internal_hom}.
2444	\end{definition}
2445	
2446	\begin{definition}
2447	
2448	\leanok
2449	[Presheaf internal hom of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules]
2450	  \label{def:presheaf_internal_hom}
2451	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.InternalHom.internalHom}
2452	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, def:presheaf_internal_hom_value,
2453	        def:presheaf_internal_hom_slice_value, lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction}
2454	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Modules on Ringed Spaces", \S Internal Hom
2455	  % (\texttt{section-internal-hom}, tag area 01CM)
2456	  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L3500--L3524).
2457	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
2458	  % "Let $(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$ be a ringed space.
2459	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$, $\mathcal{G}$ be $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules.
2460	  % Consider the rule
2461	  % $$
2462	  % U \longmapsto \Hom_{\mathcal{O}_X|_U}(\mathcal{F}|_U, \mathcal{G}|_U).
2463	  % $$
2464	  % It follows from the discussion in Sheaves, Section
2465	  % \ref{sheaves-section-glueing-sheaves} that this is a sheaf of

2485	  Let \(R\) be a presheaf of commutative rings on \(\Opens X\) and let
2486	  \(M, N \in \mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R\). The \emph{presheaf internal hom}
2487	  \(\mathcal{H}om(M, N)\) is the presheaf of \(R\)-modules whose value on an open
2488	  \(U\) is the module of morphisms of restricted modules
2489	  \[
2490	    \mathcal{H}om(M, N)(U)
2491	      \;:=\;
2492	    \mathtt{ModuleCat.of}\;\bigl(R(U)\bigr)\;\bigl(M|_U \longrightarrow N|_U\bigr),
2493	  \]
2494	  the \(R(U)\)-module of \(R|_U\)-linear morphisms \(M|_U \to N|_U\) of presheaves of
2495	  modules on \(U\), formed via the open-immersion restriction \(U \hookrightarrow X\).
2496	  This value module is the slice specialisation
2497	  \cref{def:presheaf_internal_hom_slice_value} of the morphism module
2498	  \cref{def:presheaf_internal_hom_value}.

2500	  Explicitly, \(\mathcal{H}om(M, N)\) is the presheaf of \(R\)-modules assembled from
2501	  three pieces. \emph{(a) Value modules.} On each open \(U\) the value is the
2502	  \(R(U)\)-module \((M|_U \to N|_U)\) of \cref{def:presheaf_internal_hom_value} /
2503	  \cref{def:presheaf_internal_hom_slice_value}. \emph{(b) Restriction maps.} For an
2504	  inclusion \(V \subseteq U\) the restriction map
2505	  \(\mathcal{H}om(M, N)(U) \to \mathcal{H}om(M, N)(V)\) is the further-restriction map
2506	  \cref{lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction}, \(\varphi \mapsto \varphi|_V\), which
2507	  is additive and semilinear over the ring restriction \(R(g) : R(U) \to R(V)\)
2508	  induced by \(g : V \to U\) --- the compatibility datum that the presheaf-of-modules
2509	  assembly (\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.mk}\), via \(\mathtt{ofPresheaf}\)) consumes.
2510	  \emph{(c) Functoriality.} Restriction along the identity \(U \to U\) is the identity
2511	  map, and restriction along a composite \(V \xrightarrow{g} U \xrightarrow{h} W\) of
2512	  inclusions \(V \subseteq U \subseteq W\) (the morphism \(g : V \to U\) followed by
2513	  \(h : U \to W\) in \(\mathcal{C} = \Opens X\)) is the composite of the two
2514	  restriction maps \(\mathcal{H}om(M,N)(W) \to \mathcal{H}om(M,N)(U) \to
2515	  \mathcal{H}om(M,N)(V)\); both identity and composition are inherited from the
2516	  functoriality of further restriction, since \(\mathtt{Over.map}\) is a functor. These three pieces
2517	  make \(\mathcal{H}om(M, N)\) a genuine, covariant presheaf of \(R\)-modules; this
2518	  assembled presheaf is the construction target \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.internalHom}\).

2520	  The slice formula is forced by contravariance: the pointwise rule
2521	  \(U \mapsto \Hom_{R(U)}(M(U), N(U))\) varies \emph{against} the restriction maps of
2522	  \(M\) and so is not a covariant presheaf, whereas the module of morphisms of
2523	  \emph{restricted} objects \(M|_U \to N|_U\) restricts covariantly. Thus, unlike the
2524	  tensor product \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} --- which is covariant in the
2525	  restriction maps and sheafifies directly --- the internal hom must be built through
2526	  the slice presentation, and there is no presheaf-level dual to sheafify naively.
2527	\end{definition}

2558	  \(M|_U \to N|_U\) to its further restriction \(M|_V \to N|_V\). Concretely, the

2562	  map is additive, and it is \emph{semilinear} over the ring restriction
2563	  \(R(g) : R(U) \to R(V)\): for \(f \in R(U)\),
2564	  \[

2575	  morphism \(M|_U \to N|_U\) to a morphism \(M|_V \to N|_V\) and respects identities and
2576	  composites; in particular the induced map on morphism groups is additive. For the
2577	  semilinearity, recall that the global-scalar action of
2578	  \cref{def:presheaf_internal_hom_value} acts on \(\varphi\) by post-composition with

2580	  from \(U\) to \(V\) is multiplication by \(f|_V = R(g)(f)\) on \(N|_V\). Since further
2581	  restriction commutes with composition of morphisms, restricting \(f \cdot \varphi =

2696	  \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) with \(N\) a sheaf of modules. Then the
2697	  presheaf internal hom \(\mathcal{H}om(M, N)\) of \cref{def:presheaf_internal_hom}
2698	  satisfies the sheaf condition: for an open cover \(\{U_i\}\) of \(U\), a family of
2699	  morphisms \(M|_{U_i} \to N|_{U_i}\) agreeing on the overlaps
2700	  \(U_i \cap U_j\) glues to a unique morphism \(M|_U \to N|_U\), because \(N\) is a
2701	  sheaf and morphisms of sheaves of modules are determined and gluable section-wise.
2702	  Hence \(\mathcal{H}om(M, N)\) descends to an object of \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\).
2703	  Specialising to \(N = \mathcal{O}_X = \mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\,R\) gives the
2704	  \emph{sheaf-level dual}
2705	  \[

2707	    \;\in\; \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X,
2708	  \]

2713	\begin{proof}
2714	  \uses{def:presheaf_dual, lem:internal_hom_eval}
2715	  Fix an open \(U\) and an open cover \(\{U_i\}\) of \(U\). A morphism
2716	  \(M|_U \to N|_U\) is a section of \(\mathcal{H}om(M, N)\) over \(U\); restricting it
2717	  to the \(U_i\) gives a compatible family. Conversely, given morphisms
2718	  \(\varphi_i : M|_{U_i} \to N|_{U_i}\) with \(\varphi_i|_{U_i \cap U_j} =
2719	  \varphi_j|_{U_i \cap U_j}\), section-wise gluing in the sheaf \(N\) produces, for
2720	  each open \(W \subseteq U\) and section \(s \in M(W)\), a well-defined section of
2721	  \(N(W)\) by gluing the \(\varphi_i(s|_{W \cap U_i})\) (these agree on overlaps by
2722	  hypothesis, and \(N\) is a sheaf so the glued section is unique). The resulting
2723	  assignment is \(R(W)\)-linear and natural in \(W\), hence a morphism
2724	  \(M|_U \to N|_U\) restricting to the \(\varphi_i\); uniqueness is the separatedness
2725	  of \(N\). This is exactly the sheaf condition for \(\mathcal{H}om(M, N)\), so it is a
2726	  sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. Taking \(N = \mathcal{O}_X\) yields
2727	  \(\mathtt{dual}\,M\), and the evaluation morphism of \cref{lem:internal_hom_eval},
